steps.title: Set up
steps.content: The boundary line passes through two readable points: $(0, -11)$ and $(-7, -10)$. Find the slope: $$m = \frac{-10-(-11)}{-7-0} = \frac{1}{-7} = -\frac{1}{7}$$ With y-intercept $-11$, the line is $y = -\frac{1}{7}x - 11$. Multiply through by 7: $7y = -x - 77$, so $x + 7y = -77$.

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: The shaded region is above the line, so the inequality is $x + 7y \geq -77$. Matching to $rx + ty \geq -77$: $r = 1$ and $t = 7$, giving $r + t = 1 + 7 = 8$.
